meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use project management to overcome having no portfolio. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first app.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' dilemma, build a functional Project Management tool. By documenting the development lifecycle using Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you demonstrate actual engineering proficiency. This shifts your narrative from 'student' to 'builder,' proving you can deliver production-ready software, not just code snippets.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of junior careers. You have the theoretical knowledge, but employers aren't looking for grades; they are looking for evidence of delivery. The gap between 'knowing how to code' and 'shipping a product' is where most students stall, trapped in the cycle of endless tutorials that never result in a tangible asset.

But here is the secret: you don't need a groundbreaking startup idea to get hired. You need a project management tool that mirrors the complexity of real-world enterprise software. By building a system that organizes tasks, tracks progress, and manages state, you demonstrate architectural maturity that separates you from the thousands of other applicants with identical to-do lists.

steps.title: Architecting the Database Schema
steps.description: Define the relationship between users, boards, and tasks. This establishes data integrity, a core skill for any backend developer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational schema for a project management SaaS. Include tables for users, projects, tasks, and status labels with proper foreign key constraints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema based on the Antigravity architecture output'

steps.title: Implementing Authentication Flow
steps.description: Secure your app by implementing role-based access control. This proves you understand the critical importance of user security.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an authentication flow specification using NextAuth.js, including secure session management and middleware for protected dashboard routes.

steps.title: Developing the Kanban Core
steps.description: Create the drag-and-drop interface that manages task states. This highlights your ability to handle complex frontend state management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the logic for a Kanban board component where tasks can be moved between columns, updating the database status field via optimistic UI updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx --apply-logic='implement drag-and-drop state sync'

steps.title: Adding Real-time Notifications
steps.description: Implement event-driven updates. This demonstrates your proficiency with modern web sockets and asynchronous event handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design an event-driven notification system that triggers an alert when a task status changes, using WebSockets or Server-Sent Events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'setup websocket server with socket.io for real-time task updates'

steps.title: Writing Deployment Documentation
steps.description: Professional documentation is the hallmark of a senior engineer. You must explain how to deploy your app to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional README.md that includes installation instructions, environment variable setup, and a high-level architecture diagram in Mermaid.js.

pro_tips: Always include a 'Lessons Learned' section in your README; interviewers value self-reflection more than perfect code.
Use GitHub Actions to automate testing; it shows you understand CI/CD pipelines before you even get your first job.
Document your failures—if a feature broke, explain why and how you fixed it in your commit messages.
Keep your database schema visible in your repository root; it's the first thing senior engineers look for to judge your architectural clarity.
Add a 'Contributing' file to your project; even if you're the only contributor, it signals you think in terms of team-based software engineering.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel or Railway, add the link to your LinkedIn profile, and prepare to walk through your code architecture in your first technical interview.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build a project that turns heads. The difference between you and the competition is the quality of your execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
